From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

UK parliamentary by-election

The **1958 Shoreditch and Finsbury by-election** was a UK parliamentary [by-election](/wiki/By-election "By-election") for the [Shoreditch and Finsbury](/wiki/Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Shoreditch and Finsbury (UK Parliament constituency)") constituency held on 27 November 1958.

The by-election was held following the conferment of a [life peerage](/wiki/Life_peerage "Life peerage") for [Victor Collins](/wiki/Victor_Collins,_Baron_Stonham "Victor Collins, Baron Stonham").

It was a [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") hold. Until the [1999 Leeds Central by-election](/wiki/1999_Leeds_Central_by-election "1999 Leeds Central by-election"), the turnout was the lowest in post-war history.

## Result

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1958_Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_by-election&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Result")]

Shoreditch and Finsbury by-election, 1958

| Party | | Candidate | Votes | % | ±% |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **[Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)")** | **[Michael Cliffe](/wiki/Michael_Cliffe "Michael Cliffe")** | **10,215** | **76.04** | **+2.59** |
|  | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | Thomas Henry Martin Whipham | 3,219 | 23.96 | −2.59 |
| [Majority](/wiki/Majority#Related_terms "Majority") | | | 6,996 | 52.08 | +5.18 |
| [Turnout](/wiki/Voter_turnout "Voter turnout") | | | 13,434 |  |  |
|  | [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") **hold** | | [Swing](/wiki/Swing_(politics) "Swing (politics)") |  |  |
